operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add comprehensive executable oracle cases that verify the profile's structure, mathematical properties, and file output integrity, ensuring the solution is robust against common errors.
operator_instances.rationale: The original task only has a single seed case with basic assertions. To increase verification complexity, we add multiple scaled cases that check: (1) the profile is monotonically decreasing, (2) all probabilities are valid (0-1), (3) the list is not the same object on repeated calls, (4) the JSON file is valid and matches the returned profile, and (5) edge-case-like calls (e.g., verifying first and last values strictly).

new_checks: Profile is monotonically non-increasing: profile[i] >= profile[i+1] for i in range(11)
All values are between 0 and 1 inclusive
Repeated calls return different list objects (no shared reference)
JSON file content when loaded equals the returned profile
First value strictly equals 1, last value strictly equals 0.0005
expected_failure_modes: Hardcoded list that is not monotonically decreasing
Returning the same list object each call (reference leak)
JSON file missing or incorrect content

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify the profile is monotonically non-increasing.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: Each successive element must be <= the previous element.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: A monotonically increasing or constant profile would fail.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Check monotonic decreasing property of the returned list.

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that the function returns a new list object on each call (immutable output).
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: Calling get_default_error_profile() twice must yield distinct list objects.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: If the function returns a global list reference, both calls will return the same object.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Ensure repeated calls do not share the same list object.

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Verify that the JSON file artifact contains exactly the same profile as returned.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: Reading error_profile.json gives a list identical to the return value.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: If the file is written with different values or format, equality will fail.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Validate JSON file content against returned profile.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must cover monotonicity, list uniqueness, and file-content matching.
